channel.title: Booster Conference 2026 Bergen
channel.description: Recent content on Booster Conference 2026 Bergen

item.title: Avoid the usability cliff. Create a stairway to heavenly complexity.
item.description:
“Simple things should be simple, complex things should be possible” – Alan Kay.

This is a good rule of thumb for anyone creating digital tools, no matter if you are working on the API-level or with the user interface. But it is only a start. You need to make sure that the path from simple to complex is a good stairway and not a steep cliff, making sure that the increase in user effort matches the increase in perceived value.

item.title: Bærekraftige valg – Finn tiltak som gjør løsningen din grønnere og raskere
item.description:
Visste du at 80 % av de digitale utslippene fra produktet ditt bestemmes allerede i designfasen? Våre valg som designere og utviklere har derfor en direkte innvirkning på de digitale løsningene vi skaper. Uten bevissthet rundt disse valgene kan løsningene våre bli unødvendig energikrevende – uten at vi er klar over det.

For å møte denne utfordringen har vi laget en workshop som hjelper deg med å kartlegge, vurdere og prioritere tiltak for å gjøre løsningene dine grønnere. Workshopen guider deg gjennom hvilke tiltak fra Web Sustainability Guidelines (WSG) som er aktuelt fort ditt produkt gjennom et beslutningstreet. Den hjelper deg også prioritere hvilke tiltak som er gir mest effekt i forhold til innsats for å virkelig kickstarte og vise verdien av bærekraftsarbeidet.

item.title: Be om historier
item.description:
Nøkkelen til å bygge gode produkter er god innsikt. Men dersom vi spør brukerne direkte om hva de ønsker seg, får vi ofte svar som egentlig ikke stemmer. I stedet bør vi be brukerne fortelle historier som gir oss innsikt i deres faktiske atferd.

I denne workshop’en får du hands-on erfaring med historiebasert intervjuteknikk. Du vil lære hvordan du skal stille riktige spørsmål, og hvordan du skal hjelpe brukeren til å huske og fortelle historien du er ute etter.

item.title: Blue Screen of the Brain – om utbrenthet og veien tilbake
item.description:
Utbrenthet - du tror ikke det kan skje med deg, for du elsker jo jobben din, trives med ørten baller i luften og med å ha hektiske og tettpakkede arbeidsdager. Helt til du plutselig er langtidssykemeldt og sengeliggende og ikke helt skjønner hva som skjedde.

Dette er fortellingen om min egen personlige systemkrasj - fra 100% engasjert til 0% energi. Jeg deler ærlig -og med et lite glimt i øyet - om hva som egentlig skjer i hodet og kroppen når man blir utbrent og opp- og nedturene på veien tilbake til meg selv igjen.

item.title: Booster Battle
item.description:
Two heavyweights, with wildly different and hot takes about our industry, will face off in a battle of wits, guts and glory!

Be prepared for the bout of the century, the rumble in the concrete jungle, a fight of butterflies and bees!

item.title: Build Your Own AI Agent with Model Context Protocol (MCP)
item.description:
Curious about how modern AI agents can connect to external tools and services? Join this hands-on workshop and learn to build and extend an AI agent system using the Model Context Protocol (MCP)!

You’ll get a practical introduction to MCP architecture and discover how to develop your own “tools” that empower your agent—from weather data and news to calendar integration and notifications. Learn how the agent leverages OpenAI, how to containerize everything with Docker, and how to easily expand the system with new features.

item.title: Bygg en tilbakemeldingskultur – fra teori til praksis
item.description: I denne interaktive workshopen utforsker vi hvordan tilbakemeldinger kan bli en naturlig og verdifull del av hverdagen i teknologimiljøer. Mange utviklere og konsulenter er sterke på fag, men mangler verktøy og trygghet når det kommer til kommunikasjon og personlig utvikling. Vi tar for oss hvorfor tilbakemeldinger er en kritisk soft skill, og hvordan man kan trene på å både gi og motta dem – på en måte som bygger relasjoner og fremmer læring.

item.title: Can we architecture our way out of a chaotic reality?
item.description:
In the IT industry we have this idea that we can remove our systems from the chaos of real life, either through careful consideration of architecture or attention to detail in modelling. Thus we should create simple, or at least maintainable systems.

We all know this isn’t what we face in our professional lives, where even the seemingly smallest detail can cause huge delays and complications.

Join me in viewing system development and evolving systems through the lens of the chaos of reality and societal development.

item.title: code in Unison
item.description:
Unison, a friendly programming language from the future, offers a few interesting new insights into what programming could be.

Most notable they are

  • a new way of developing and building code
  • seemingly seamless way of distributed programming
  • a novel means of managing effects via abilities.

In this workshop we take you on a journey where we explore the finer details of these promises. You will get to know Unison, its programming model and the ease with which you can describe and deploy distributed programs.

Deployment i 2025 - serverløs i praksis
item.description: Deploy pleide å bety pipelines, CI-skript og YAML-filer. I dag kan det være så enkelt som å trykke på en knapp. Jeg skal vise hvordan moderne infrastukturplatformer som Vercel har gjort det mulig å shippe en full webapp på minutter, uten å tenke på servere, infrastruktur eller DevOps.

item.title: Dismantling the Speed/Quality Trade-off Fallacy with Systems Thinking
item.description:
The speed / quality trade-off fallacy gets many teams stuck in negative feedback loops - sacrificing quality for speed, both are lost.

Teams find their backlogs filled with bugs, their stakeholders frustrated by missed deadlines and the teams themselves unhappy and stressed.

Systems thinking, and in particular using causal loop diagrams to identify the feedback loops at play can shed light on how and why the fallacy is so tempting yet so destructive.

item.title: Don't panic! The Hitchhiker's guide to the terminal
item.description: Once upon a time the terminal was the primary way to engage with computers. In our time graphical user interfaces reign supreme and many people can get by perfectly fine without ever using the terminal. And yet, there may come a time when you will have to use it. And I am aiming to prepare you for these times. What is the difference between a terminal and a shell? How do you navigate your files from the terminal? These questions and many more shall be answered in this hands-on exploration of the terminal. If you are curious about or intimidated by the terminal, this is the workshop for you.

item.title: Effektivisering av velferdstaten - ett skjema om gangen
item.description:
NAV er en av Norges største arbeidsgivere og er ansvarlige for å opprettholde mye av den norske velferdsstaten. I dette foredraget vil jeg prate om hvordan vi designet, utviklet og kontinuerlig forbedret den digitale søknaden på barnetrygd fra en tidligere papirbasert løsning.

Foredraget beskriver fordelene og utfordringene vi møtte da vi skulle digitalisere en analog og papirbasert søknadsprosess, hvordan vi samlet både kvalitativ og kvantitativ innsikt som en del av denne prosessen, og hvordan vi målte resultatene av arbeidet. Jeg vil snakke om de konkrete målene vi nådde, hvordan du kan bruke kvantitativ måling både til å måle suksess og som et prioriteringsverktøy, og reflektere over hvordan arbeidet bidro til å gi innbyggerne i Norge bedre digitale tjenester.

item.title: Empati-Lab
item.description:
Vi er to UX-designere med et sterkt engasjement for empati og menneskesentrert design.

Workshopen starter med en kort introduksjon til universell utforming, og hvorfor det er et viktig verktøy for å skape verdi for både mennesker og virksomheter. Deretter inviterer vi deltakerne inn i Empati-lab, en praktisk workshop der man får erfare, ikke bare forstå, hvordan ulike funksjonsnedsettelser påvirker hverdagen.

Denne workshopen passer for alle som vil lære mer om universell utforming og oppleve andre type brukerbehov.

item.title: Event Sourcing: From Chaos to Control
item.description:
Har du noen gang jobbet på et prosjekt hvor historikken er visket ut? Ti år har gått, ingen fra det originale teamet er igjen, og ingen vet hvorfor dataen ser ut som den gjør. Vi har alle vært der. Vi lagrer dagens tilstand, men mister hele reisen – all den verdifulle konteksten om hva som har skjedd over tid.

Hva om vi kunne endre det? Hva om applikasjonen din ikke bare visste hva tilstanden er nå, men også hvordan den kom dit? Dette høres kanskje ut som magi, men det er mulig med Event Sourcing! I denne workshoppen introduserer vi konsepter rundt Event Sourcing og hvordan det kan brukes til å orkestrere en nettbutikk. Vi vil lære å definere eventer, opprette og lytte på event-strømmer, og hvordan man kan bygge opp en database-tilstand fra eventene.

item.title: Fjelltoppjakten - Vedlikeholdsestimering er nøkkelen til hobbyprosjekter
item.description:
Fjelltoppjakten er ikke bare en app. Det er en hobby. Det er en livsidentitet. Ja, flere tusen nordmenn planlegger sommerferien hvert år utifra hvilke topper som skal sjekkes av i Fjelltoppjakten.

Hvordan kan to bergensere utvikle og vedlikeholde en app med 130 000 brukere og millioninntekt, år etter år, på fritiden?

Vi vil gjerne fortelle historien om Fjelltoppjakten, og hvordan vedlikeholdsestimering kan endre hvordan du tenker på produktutvikling.

item.title: From Curiosity to Clarity: How to Talk to Users and Learn What Matters
item.description:
Everyone says “talk to your users”, but what does that actually look like in practice?

In this interactive workshop, you’ll experience design thinking in action through the classic Wallet Project exercise, while learning how UX research strengthens every step of the process. Working in pairs, you’ll interview, synthesize insights, and rapidly design for a real user need.

Along the way, you’ll learn: How to plan and run effective user interviews How design thinking moves from empathy to ideation to action When to use different research methods How to test ideas and interpret what you learn

item.title: From UX Research to Reality: Collaborating to Build Products That Work
item.description:
Great products aren’t built by UX research alone. They’re built when researchers, designers, product managers and developers collaborate around real user needs and technical constraints.

In this talk, I’ll share practical learnings from my journey as a solo UX researcher on how to scale research impact through collaboration, and how this directly contributes to building products that are desirable for users, feasible to deliver, and viable for the business.

You’ll learn how research can fit into everyday product delivery, how non-researchers can start thinking like users, and what effective collaboration actually looks like across disciplines.

item.title: Frontend frameworks free-for-all!
item.description:
Bring that shiny frontend framework that you have been wanting to try out. I’ll bring some data, an API, and a sketch of what we will be building. Let’s try something new!

React is everywhere nowadays, but there are so many options that does at least something better, or different. Maybe you want to try out two way data binding in Vue, enterprise frontend in Angular, make something small and fast (and without the VDOM) with svelte, resumable apps on the edge with qwik, islands with deno and fresh, or HTML as it should have been with htmx?

item.title: Fun with F# and turtle geometry
item.description:
Turtle geometry is made from the viewpoint of a small turtle walking on an infinitely sized sheet of paper, leaving a trace where it walks. The turtle’s movements can be controlled by a small set of commands, like “move forward” and “turn”.

In this workshop you will learn basic functional programming with F#, while creating geometry. We will explore algebraic data types, pattern matching, functions and recursion, all to make the turtle move. We will make polygons, fractals and space filling curves, and look into some useful theorems for these geometric shapes. You can choose to concentrate on the F# tasks at hand, or you can allow yourself to be distracted by the beautiful geometry, and continue to explore different variants of the shapes.

item.title: Hands-on prototyping med og for KI
item.description:
KI har endret spillereglene for hvordan vi jobber. Etter en periode med både hype og skuffelser, begynner vi å forstå hvor teknologien virkelig gir verdi for designere: I prototyping! I denne workshopen får du hands-on erfaring med hvordan du kan bruke KI til å bygge prototyper, og hvordan vi kan lage prototyper av KI tjenester.

Som mange har erfart, er tradisjonelle designverktøy ofte begrensende. Prototypene blir enkle og brukertester handler mest om å validere våre planlagte flyter. Men når vi kobler på KI, kan vi skape prototyper hvor brukerne selv får utforske løsningen, følge sin egen flyt og vi kan observere hvordan de faktisk forstår og opplever tjenesten.

item.title: Hvordan dra nytte av egne data med KI?
item.description: Brage og Nora leder dere gjennom en utforskende workshop hvor dere jobber i små grupper med utgangspunkt i realistiske løsninger fra norske virksomheter. Sammen undersøker vi hvordan kunstig intelligens kan løse reelle utfordringer og gi merverdi for produkter, tjenester og sluttbrukere. Med Design Thinking som metode går vi fra problem til idé, og “koker sammen” skisser som kan løse hverdagsproblemer eller gi merverdi for din bedrift og deres kunder. Her får du inspirasjon og konkrete metoder du kan ta med hjem!

item.title: It is a sociotechnical system - but what does that mean?
item.description:
Sociotechnical systems theory was made based on the discovery that different people use technology different. The first research report was from coalmines where new technology that was introduced made the work go slower. But then, they discovered one mine who managed to use the technology in a more efficient way, and this is an important discovery.

Because, if people work differently with the same technology, then we need to understand how to design good work. That means that we need to have design principles, not only for designing technology but for designing work systems consisting of people and technology.

item.title: Keep up, without crashing out
item.description: We live in a world of change. We are constantly facing new technology, roles and responsibilities are changing and it’s easy to feel that you’re either falling behind or getting burned out. At conferences we learn lots of new stuff that we would like to try out. At the same time we need to deliver high quality in our regular jobs. How? Demanding that all professional development comes on top of our normal workload or needs to happen in our free time is not sustainable.

item.title: Lock picking for fun and absolutely no profit
item.description:
Have you ever wondered how the lock on your front door actually works? This workshop offers a hands-on introduction to the inner workings of common locks. We’ll explore how pins, tumblers, and other mechanisms interact, and then move from theory to practice as participants try their hand at opening a number of padlocks on their own.

The focus of this workshop is on learning, curiosity, and a deeper appreciation of everyday objects we take for granted. By the end of the session, you’ll understand what makes locks tick, where their strengths and weaknesses lie, and why design details matter. All participants will be provided with a training lock and a set of lock picking tools to continue exploring on their own after the workshop.

item.title: Make a Programming Language!
item.description:
Write your own programming language and learn more about how they work behind the scenes.

In this workshop we’ll be building an interpreter for a simple programming language. You’ll be able to put your own spin on the language as we develop it. This can give you some insight into the different design choices that can be made when developing a language, and why current programming languages look the way they do.

item.title: MCP: USB-C for AI or a Shortcut to Skynet?
item.description:
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is often described as USB-C for AI: a standard way to connect models to tools, data, and systems. As language models become more capable and more connected, MCP makes it easier to build systems that can do much more than just generate text.

That shift is exciting, but it also raises difficult questions about security, safety, and trust. In this talk, I’ll use MCP as a lens to explore what changes when AI systems start acting beyond the chat window, and why that should make us think harder about control, authority and what we’re actually willing to trust these systems with.

item.title: Methodology as a Straitjacket
item.description:
At some point, we started treating methodology like religion. Agile became dogma. Design Thinking turned into ritual. Double Diamond became an icon people worship without fully understanding what it means. Everyone talks about “doing it right”, but very few remember why we are doing any of it in the first place.

People fill out canvases like they are sitting an exam, and panic when reality refuses to fit the template. Teams get stuck in ceremonies they actively dislike, but keep doing them because “it says so in the book”. And when someone finally asks, “Does this actually make sense?”, they are labelled difficult.

item.title: Next year, you're the speaker!
item.description:
There is no better way to attend a conference than being a speaker. You get a free ticket, bragging rights, and best of all, the conference will treat you like royalty. You might even be invited to a fancy speakers’ dinner.

But let’s be honest: it’s terrifying! “I have nothing to talk about!” “I don’t know where to start!” “What if I forget everything and just stand there!”

The purpose of this workshop is to help you get rid of those excuses. It doesn’t matter if you’ve held a talk before, or this will be your very first. You don’t need a to bring a talk idea. If you don’t think anyone would want to hear what you have to say, this is very much for you.

item.title: Open spaces
item.description: A self organizing conference. QA your favorite speakers. Listen in to interesting conversations. Or dive into a passionate (and of course respectful) debate!

item.title: På kanten av blackout: Fleksibel strømstyring i sanntid med IoT-enheter
item.description:
Hver gang du skrur på lyset hjemme, må strømforbruk og -produksjon balanseres i sanntid for å unngå strømbrudd. I denne presentasjonen viser jeg hvordan kantprosessering og smarte styringssystemer gjør det mulig for batterier å bidra til å stabilisere strømnettet, og holde lysene på.

Jeg vil dele hvordan vi har designet IoT riggen vår på Azure som muliggjør automatisk innrullering av nye kantenheter, datainnsamling fra sensorer, fjernstyring av elektrisk infrastruktur og kjøring av sanntidsalgoritmer for strømstyring på kanten når det er ekstra behov for pålitelighet og rask reaksjonsevne.

item.title: Practice discovering and talking about unpredictability
item.description:
Hvorfor Stadig opplever team som driver med digital produktutvikling at de blir revet mellom å levere basert på forventninger om forutsigbarhet, og behovet for å utforske og levere det som de har funnet ut vil gi verdi. Vi har erfart at det nytter å snakke om dette med interessentene ved hjelp av Cynefin.

Hva skal vi lære?

  • Bli kjent med Cynefin rammeverket. Det er en modell som vi har god erfaring med, som folk kan kjenne seg igjen i.
  • Utfordring: interessentene ønsker ofte å vite hva noe koster og hvor lang tid det tar for å bli ferdig, men det er ikke alltid vi klarer å møte det behovet fordi mye av det vi lager i systemutvikling er ukjent.
  • Hvordan kan vi snakke om det ukjente, og hvordan kan vi jobbe og kontrollere arbeidet.

Plan for workshop Intro til Cynefin (Kunnskap) Workshop: setter seg i par/ grupper og definerer noen arbeidsoppgaver innenfor Cynefin-rammeverket (Øvelse) Deling og refleksjon i plenum (Refleksjon)

item.title: Praktisk Domain-Driven Design - Fokus på modellering
item.description:
Uansett hva slags rolle du har i digitale produktutviklingen er du involvert i modellering. Det går alltid en grense mellom fysiske omgivelser og software produkt and den grensen defineres av modellen som produktet velger til å beskrive virkeligheten.

I denne sesjonen vil du øve deg på modellering for å kunne bli bedre til å designe, lede og bygge digitale produkter.

Målet er å inspirere deg til å tenke fra ulike perspektive og til å foreslå ulike modeller for samme problemet du skal løse. Vi vil gå gjennom 4 forskjellige tilnærminger til modellering, der hvert steg tilfører mer nyanse og detalj i forståelsen av problemområdet:

item.title: Reclaiming Digital Sovereignty
item.description:
For several decades, we’ve allowed ourselves to become deeply dependent on US technologies, platforms, and digital services. The alliance between Silicon Valley and the Trump administration has shown us how that choice made us vulnerable.

Paris Marx will discuss how we became so dependent on US tech and what it might look like to chart of different course. Getting off US tech won’t be easy, but it presents an opportunity to reimagine how we use digital technology for the better.

item.title: Red light, Green light!
item.description:
Join our workshop where we dive into cutting edge computer vision models and how they can be used to create your very own rendition of the classic schoolyard game “Red light, Green light”.

Participants will learn how to harness a smartphone’s camera, process a live video feed, and use a pre-trained pose estimation model to detect movement.

Prerequisites:

  • A laptop
  • A smartphone with cable that connects to your laptop.

To make the initial setup more smooth it is also advised to go through the setup guide for Flutter.

item.title: Relax with Pixel Art IRL
item.description: Take a break from your screen and create some Pixel Art for your desk. Make your favorite character, programming language mascot or whatever you like from iron-fusible beads and take home your very own desk ornament.

item.title: Robust and idempotent build pipelines using nix
item.description: Build pipelines can be challenging to get right, they can be brittle and hard to debug, often suffering from ‘work on my machine’ syndrome. We can eliminate most of this headache by adding nix to the mix

item.title: Same Same, but Sustainably Different: Designing a Ferry-tale
item.description: What happens when you take design lessons from car dashboards and try to steer a ferry toward lower emissions? This talk dives into how familiar eco-feedback principles from driving don’t always sail smoothly into maritime contexts, and what we learn when they don’t. Discover how small contextual shifts can completely change design assumptions, and why crossing domains can spark both creativity and caution. A short story about designing for sustainability, learning from other industries, and knowing when to stop following the map and start reading the sea.

item.title: SQL Mystery - Murder at Bryggen
item.description: Want to freshen up your SQL skills? How long has it been since you wrote some direct and complex queries, and not only joins or simple selections? Time to change that. Inspired by the OG SQL Murder Mystery (by Northwestern University Knight Lab), we have created a scenario in Bergen, and it is your job to solve the murder. Your tools? SQL queries. Let’s see if your detective skills are sufficient to find out who did the murder! Maybe there are twists to the tale that you need to solve?

item.title: The future of design is here and you can’t see it
item.description:
From the 1980s until today, design has gone from not matter, to everything that matters, to not matter again. The paradigm of design seems to be changing faster than what we designers have managed to understand. What happens when we do not have any interfaces to design for? And who is actually making the design decisions for our products?

In this lightning talk we will look at how digital design has changed both visually and conceptually in the last 4 decades and look at the key factors of what designing means in the (artificial) intelligence paradigm of the future!

item.title: The Future of SEO When AI Provides the Answers
item.description:
As more searches end without a click and AI delivers answers directly across search results and AI chat, the rules of SEO are changing. It’s no longer just about ranking first, but about becoming a source AI uses in its answers.

In this talk, you’ll learn how to optimize websites and content for both humans and machines, and how to approach SEO in a world where the user journey often starts (and sometimes ends) in the search results.

item.title: There is more to life than KPIs
item.description:
During an Open Space discussion at last year`s Booster Conference, we discussed the distance between leaders and teams. I think the gap is real, but it is not there because of bad intentions. Instead, I believe the gap exists because it is difficult to see how leaders achieve control by letting go of it.

Over the past year leading an area of highly skilled and diverse teams, I have put this into practice. Some things worked, others didn’t, but the common thread is that I must prove myself worthy of the team’s trust.

item.title: Uenighet kan være gull for å forbedre design
item.description:
I denne workshopen dykker vi ned i hvorfor friksjon og motstand ikke er noe vi skal unngå, men faktisk kan være drivkraften bak bedre idéer, samarbeid og løsninger.

Mange av oss er konfliktsky og allergisk mot dårlig stemning, noe som fort kan stå i veien for ærlig samarbeid og bedre designprosesser. Åpen dialog og forståelse for hverandre, gjør at vi slipper unødvendig drama og kan holde tempoet oppe.

Vi skal derfor:

item.title: Utforsk scenariobygging med brettspillet Strategisløyfen
item.description:
Har du hørt andre snakke om scenariobygging, men ikke testet det selv?

I denne workshopen får du lære mer om metodene bak scenariobygging, hva du bør gjøre før du starter, og hva du bør bruke scenarioene til. Brettspillet tar deg gjennom en produktstrategi-prosess med scenariobygging steg for steg. Spillet går ut på å planlegge oppgaver og innsats godt med bruk av spillkort og tokens.

Har du husket alt, eller må du ta to skritt tilbake og trekke et nytt kort?

item.title: When the cloud came home, rethinking code and consequences
item.description:
After years of focusing on clean architecture and efficient code, I had an eye-opening moment when plans emerged to build a massive data center down my street. It made me realize that, despite working in tech for over a decade, I had rarely questioned the environmental or societal impact of the systems I help create. Like many, I was a skilled builder but also a passive consumer of infrastructure.

In this talk, I will explore how everyday choices in code, cloud infrastructure, and system design affect far more than we realize, from security and GDPR risks to energy consumption and environmental impact. I’ll share why developers must take an active role in questioning cloud defaults, promoting sovereignty, and building greener, more responsible digital systems.

item.title: When the narcissist runs the show
item.description: There is a lot of talk about good and bad leadership. But what about toxic leadership? What happens when a leader doesn’t just fall short — but actively damages culture, erodes trust, and poisons the work environment? And what does that mean for the people affected and for the organization itself?

item.title: You too can build you own smart mirror!
item.description:
A few years ago some people at work built a smart mirror for use at a conference. Maybe you’ve seen it? It had face recognition, personalized content, weather forecast, bus schedule, and so on.

Since there was nothing stopping us, we followed up by building smaller versions to bring home with us, and mine quickly became a popular “member of the family”.

Now I will show you how easily you too can build your own smart mirror!

item.title: A celebration of Teletext: The first digital information platform
item.description:
Teletext was more than just text and numbers on a TV screen. It was an early digital platform that delivered live information directly into people’s living rooms.

In this lightning talk, we look at how Teletext worked, from data hidden in the TV signal to the broadcast model that made real time information available decades before the internet.

When NRK shut down its Teletext service on 20 August 2025 after 42 years on air, Norway did not just lose a text service. It lost a real time broadcast information platform.

item.title: A close reading of Computer Machinery and Intelligence
item.description:
In the age of the LLM, the Turing Test is more relevant than ever. But what is the Turing Test? How did Turing envision the Imitation Game, as he called it, and what does he see as its greatest limitations?

In this session, Felienne will take you on wild ride through a seminal paper in computer science, philosophy and computation.

item.title: Bli med bak kulissene i utviklingen av en ny digital helseløsning
item.description:
Vi har alt man kan ønske seg for å lage et godt digitalt produkt:

En tydelig idé, støtte fra Forskningsrådet, engasjerte medisinske forskere, et kompetent utviklingsteam og en kommune som faktisk skal ta det i bruk. God tid, godt grunnlag og et viktig mål:

Å gi innbyggere råd ved nyoppstått sykdom og skade, slik at de får hjelp til egenomsorg eller vet når de må kontakte helsetjenesten.

Men hvorfor er det da fortsatt så vanskelig?

item.title: Brutalt webdesign, fra rå betong til HTML
item.description:
I denne lyntalen ser vi på hvordan brutalismens arkitektoniske prinsipper - “truth to materials”, funksjonalitet og rå estetikk, kan hjelpe oss å bygge moderne nettsider.

Etterkrigstidens brutalistiske arkitektur ble kjent for sin kompromissløse bruk av betong, eksponerte strukturer og funksjonell ærlighet. På samme måte kan brutalistisk webdesign tilby et forfriskende alternativ i dagens digitale landskap.

Vi ser på:

Brutalismens historiske opprinnelse og filosofiske grunnlag Hvordan brutalistiske prinsipper kan overføres til kode og brukergrensesnitt

item.title: Build your own cloud
item.description: In light of todays weakened trust in the “tech giants” and the focus on privacy and security, we will explore an alternative to the “spin it up in {Insert cloud provider here}” solution. In this talk, we will take a look at various open source tools and software that can be used to build a competitive hosting platform. Do we have to give up the advantages that the hyperscalers provide? Are there specific benefits to building it yourself? Is this only relevant for large companies with their own DevOps teams? These are questions we will explore in this session.

item.title: Design for endring, ikke bare engasjement.
item.description:
Gamification har lenge vært et fristende virkemiddel i design – poeng, badges og streaks som skal få brukere til å «gjøre mer». Men hva skjer når disse virkemidlene ikke fører til endring – bare aktivitet?

I dette foredraget utforsker Sofija hvordan design kan fungere som endringsledelse: Hvordan vi kan støtte reell atferdsendring, motivasjon og mestring – uten å erstatte menneskelig driv med kunstige belønninger.

Med eksempler fra helsefeltet viser hun hvordan innsikt fra atferdsdesign og endringsledelse kan brukes til å bygge løsninger som faktisk hjelper folk med å få til en endring – på sine egne premisser.

item.title: Designing Transparent Tech for Kids: Why Explainability Matters
item.description: Children today use apps that collect health data, monitor behavior, and adapt learning, often without understanding how or why. In this lightning talk, we explore the critical need for explainability in school technology. Why must tech be transparent not just for engineers, but also for students, teachers, and parents? What does it mean to design for understanding, and what are the risks when we don’t?

item.title: Do You Really Have a Team?
item.description:
Unpacking the Illusion of Teamwork in Agile Development.

Many software teams claim to be “Agile,” but in reality, they operate as loosely connected individuals rather than cohesive, high-performing teams. In this talk, we’ll explore the illusion of teamwork—how daily standups, ticket ownership, and even Agile rituals can mask siloed behavior and developer ego. Drawing from real-world examples and Agile principles, we’ll contrast dysfunctional patterns with the traits of truly collaborative teams. You’ll leave with practical strategies to foster trust, shared ownership, and team-level accountability—whether you’re a developer, Scrum Master, or leader.

item.title: Final Destination: Hvordan elektriske standarder holder deg i live, men er umulige å finne frem i.
item.description:
Høsten 2025: Stormen Amy feier inn over Vestlandet, trær rives opp med roten og strømledninger ryker. Mens mørket senker seg, ruller BKKs montørbiler ut – fulle av verktøy, kaffetermoser og RENblader: de tekniske biblene for drift og vedlikehold av elektriske anlegg i Norge.

Et år tidligere hadde REN lansert RENblad-assistenten: en chatbot som gjør det mulig å finne riktig veiledning og riktig seksjon på sekunder – midt i felten, med jord på støvlene og strømbrudd på alle kanter. Bergen får strømmen tilbake. Suksessen er et faktum.

item.title: Font for thought: set it once, say it well
item.description:
Fonts, line heights and… fewer headaches? Yes, please.

Join us for a fast-paced dive into how a solid typography system can save time, reduce team debates, and make onboarding a breeze.

Because arguing about font sizes isn’t a sprint goal!

item.title: From clueless to queueless ⛴️
item.description:
In Northern Norway, you are twice as likely to miss a ferry as anywhere else in the country. Combine that with long round-trip times and a massive summer influx of tourists, and you have a recipe for frustration — and a juicy news headline.

Today’s “first come, first served” principle does not scale in the north. Through an interdisciplinary design thinking process involving locals, tourists, ferry crews, and domain experts, we aim to reimagine the ferry journey and eliminate queues across the county.

item.title: From Legacy to Rust: A Real-World Backend Migration Story
item.description:
Rust is the most admired programming language. But is it production ready? And is it possible to hire developers? Can you convince management to use it?

This talk will cover our story at Zrch, where we use Rust as the main language in our backend. We will share our learnings, our migration approach and the challenges we overcame along the way.

What you’ll learn: • How our first attempt to rewrite legacy code failed (and why) • The strategies that made our Rust migration successful • Pain points of Rust • Interactions with other languages • Building a Rust team: hiring and onboarding challenges • Leveraging AI tools for Rust development • If time permits, we will briefly cover frontend aspects

item.title: Hvordan vi samarbeider som utvikler og designer
item.description: Kan en designer og en utvikler virkelig jobbe sømløst sammen? Dette spørsmålet dykker vi ned i under vår presentasjon, der vi deler erfaringer fra vårt samarbeid som utvikler og designer i et tverrfaglig produktteam. Mange organisasjoner beveger seg bort fra tradisjonelle vannfallsmetoder og over mot smidige, tverrfaglige team. Likevel kan det være utfordrende å få designere og utviklere til å samarbeide effektivt når man er vant til separate prosesser med overleveringer som standard arbeidsmåte.

item.title: Oops, my agent did it again
item.description: Our industry is currently excited to put large language models into everything, everywhere, all at once. As the big headline of 2025, we have put LLMs into a looping program and called them AI agents. These agents can autonomously do stuff in our environment, without human involvement, and use the model context protocol (MCP) to access tools. This development has some serious implications for security and safety, and we have some work to do to avoid a future where hackers are the ones laughing. Get an overview of how some of this technology actually works, how it fails and why it’s a big challenge to secure it.

item.title: Reckless use of AI is unethical
item.description:
AI technology is being used for everything from helping people with meal planning to programming. However, if we are not conscious of what AI is and its limitations, it could have significant consequences for our future. If we blindly trust that AI data is neutral, we risk developing products and solutions that discriminate.

This talk is not a doomsday warning, but rather a reminder of the potential consequences of using AI, along with a gentle nudge to get ahead of AI’s biases.

item.title: "Im In!": Why Movies and TV Get Tech So Hilariously Wrong
item.description:
We all love a good movie or TV show, but almost every time technology is involved, something ridiculous happens. The hacker cracks government security in 30 seconds, the CCTV cameras zoom in to read license plates and someone types furiously at a keyboard until a satellite is compromised. Why do writers keep getting the most basic of tech so wrong?

Join me as we dive into some infamous tech tropes from film and television and ask the question: “What if these scenes aren’t just a result of bad writing? What if they reflect a deeper truth: that explaining real-world tech is just really hard?”

item.title: Du er ikke Dum - En Forskningsbasert Metode for å Lære Alt Mulig
item.description:
Er du lei av å lese ting igjen og igjen, og én uke senere har du glemt alt sammen? Da gjør du én eller to vanlige feil som motvirker diin evne til å huske det du lærer.

I denne morsomme, jordnære og konkrete presentasjonen vil Peter gå gjennom de to vanligste, men dårligste metodene folk bruker når de skal lære seg ting, og forklare hvorfor de fungerer så dårlig.

I tillegg vil Peter gå gjennom fire enkle metoder som forskning har funnet er mest effektivt for å lære nye konsepter, og hvordan enkelt integrere de i en arbeidshverdag.

item.title: Easter will break your code
item.description:
Developers understand that dates can be tricky, but you might not realise how much havoc a moving holiday can wreak. Easter doesn’t have a date at all. It moves based on astronomical rules, and drags a bunch of other holidays around in its wake. That will wreck your schedules, payroll systems, and calendar apps if you mess up.

Together we will unpack the Easter algorithm, look at how it shows up in modern software, and why a 4th century decision keeps throwing curveballs at our code. You’ll walk away knowing how to calculate it and a renewed respect for how dangerous “simple” date logic really is.

item.title: Going Old School - Moving from the cloud to my own server
item.description:
How is it like going back to the good old days of early 2000, where anything ran on it’s own server?

I will share my experience of moving away from the cloud and back to a virtual private server. What can you get, and what do you lose? What does it mean in terms of deployment processes, security, robustness, scalability, privacy and compliance and performance?

…and the big question of course: Would I do it again?

item.title: Hack, Learn, Improve: Inside Storebrand’s Bug Bounty Experience
item.description:
Over the past years, Storebrand has actively engaged with ethical hackers through its bug bounty program, uncovering and mitigating vulnerabilities across a wide range of systems. In this talk, we’ll share what we’ve learned from real-world reports (from surprising edge cases to recurring patterns) and how these insights have strengthened both our technology and our culture.

We’ll highlight some of the most interesting findings, discuss how we improved our detection and response processes, and reflect on what it takes to build genuine collaboration between developers and security researchers. Whether you’re a developer, tester, or security professional, this session offers practical lessons on turning bugs into opportunities for better security.

item.title: Kreativitet kan omsettes til data (og her er hvordan)
item.description:
Har du noen gang hatt en dag hvor alt bare flyter…Idéene strømmer på, og arbeidet føles lekende lett? Eller en dag der det stikk motsatte skjer…hvor alt føles tungt, og motivasjonen er på bunn? Hva er det egentlig som driver disse svingningene?

Gjennom spennende arbeidslivsforskning har vi innsikt i hvordan indre motivasjon, stress og opplevelser påvirker kreativiteten vår – og til og med hva som skiller de mest innovative selskapene fra de som kollapser. I dette foredraget vil jeg dele både forskningsfunn og min egen erfaring med å måle min egen kreativitet gjennom et halvt år. Til slutt vil jeg dele konkrete tips til å styrke din egen kreativitet og indre motivasjon, både i arbeidslivet og i hverdagen.

item.title: Lisensen røk – Resultatet ble dataprodukter for TV 2
item.description:
Lisensen til TV 2 Play sin sentrale innholdsdatabase (Vimond) nærmet seg slutten. I stedet for å fornye en utdatert monolitt, tok vi et strategisk valg: å bygge vår egen, distribuerte arkitektur basert på dataprodukter. Slik ble Vixit-prosjektet født – en ambisiøs transformasjon for å ta tilbake eierskapet til våre data og skape smidighet.

Denne migreringen var en reise i tospann: Den skjedde parallelt med en stor omorganisering av TV 2 Digital og etableringen av ny produktorganisering. Med åtte team involvert over nesten to år, bød prosjektet på betydelige utfordringer. Vi har hatt en bratt læringskurve med Data Mesh-prinsipper, håndtert komplekse forretningsregler spredt i gamle systemer, og navigert både tekniske og kulturelle skifter.

item.title: Luksusfellen for software team: Hvordan håndterer vi teknisk gjeld?
item.description: Teknisk gjeld oppstår av mange forskjellige årsaker. Noen ganger er vi selv skyldige, andre ganger er det organisasjonen vi jobber i eller rett og slett den evige tekniske utviklingen rundt oss som fører oss ut i ulykka. Når det er forskjellige årsaker til teknisk gjeld, må vi også håndtere disse på forskjellige måter. I dette foredraget vil jeg dele erfaringer og noen verktøy og metoder som teamet ditt kan bruke for å få et mer bevisst forhold til sin egen tekniske gjeld, og hvordan man kan begynne å jobbe med den. På samme måte som i Luksusfellen handler det om å se realiteten i øynene og begynne å lage en plan!

item.title: Reglene bak et magisk design
item.description: Mange designbeslutninger er enkle å forklare, som at man må ha tydelige CTA-knapper og at det er viktig å få fokus på toppoppgavene til brukerne. Men vi designere gjør også en del valg som vi kanskje ikke er like gode på å rettferdiggjøre. Har du noen gang lurt på hvorfor det er så mye luft i designet, hvorfor teksten er plassert akkurat slik og hvorfor avstanden er større her enn der, uten å få noen gode svar fra designeren? Er du nysgjerrig på hvilke vurderinger designeren gjør underveis og hvilke designprinsipper vi bruker for å komme frem til et digg design?

item.title: Scaling Varnish on AWS - How Norway's Leading News Platform Handles Millions of Requests Per Second
item.description: Norway’s largest newspaper vg.no successfully migrated from physical data centers to Varnish Cache on AWS, implementing large-scale clusters in Kubernetes to serve millions of requests per second. Get to know more about how the migration delivered improved performance through optimized caching strategies and auto-scaling, while significantly reducing infrastructure costs and enhancing operational flexibility.

item.title: Solving the complexity of sharing test data
item.description: When testing together with other organisations one of the challenges is sharing test data. This has become more complex as the privacy rules have become stricter. We can’t share data from production copies any longer, and when everyone can create their own test data the chances that they are coordinated across different organisations will go down. I will show how we do this at Nav when testing with other government agencies and private companies. I will give an overview of resources that are available and also show one or two examples of what we have done. Even though Nav is a large organisation with plenty of resources this can fairly easily be replicated in smaller organisations.

item.title: Svarut - en kommunal kjærlighetshistorie
item.description:
SvarUt er en tjeneste de færreste har hørt om – men som nesten alle innbyggere i Norge usynlig bruker. Får du et brev fra din kommune, går det mest sannsynlig gjennom SvarUt før det havner i din digitale eller fysiske postkasse.

Tjenesten ble opprinnelig utviklet av Bergen kommune, og var blant de første som ble overtatt av KS Digital, da en del av KS, og gjort tilgjengelig for hele kommunesektoren. Det ble et viktig startpunkt for det vi jobber for i dag: Å utvikle og forvalte felles, nasjonale digitale tjenester – både ved å overta og drifte gode løsninger som sektoren har utviklet, og ved å bygge helt nye tjenester fra grunnen av.

item.title: The Coastline Paradox and Software Development
item.description: How long is a country’s coastline? It turns out that the more precise you try to measure it, the longer it becomes. But wait a minute, isn’t that just like trying to estimate a software development project? Yes, it is, and actually there’s a good reason for that too. Join this lightning talk to find out why, and what consequences this has for your next software project!

item.title: Unplug to Recharge: The Hidden Power of Off-Screen Time
item.description: After years working in PR, IT Operations, and DevOps, and spending time as a Scrum Master along the way, I’ve learned that uptime isn’t everything, especially when it comes to humans. When I started my software engineering studies at 30, I thought the key to catching up was spending more hours in front of the screen. It wasn’t. I discovered that my most creative ideas often appeared when I stepped away from the keyboard. In this lightning talk, I’ll show how taking breaks can boost creativity, sharpen focus, and bring clarity to complex problems in the fast-paced day-to-day of a developer. You’ll leave with a few simple, evidence-based habits for building rest into your schedule, because time spent off the screen isn’t lost – it is maintenance.

item.title: Cognitive load is misunderstood
item.description:
Is experiencing a high cognitive load always bad? Does it make sense to talk about cognitive load on the team level? Are we better able to think about the big problems if we offload the small ones?

Cognitive Load Theory has risen in popularity the last few years, and rightly so! Developers and architects have much to learn from psychology. But with popularity comes misunderstandings, since few ever take the time to read the actual research. To rid us of the misunderstandings I will take you through the concepts you can use to develop a clearer vocabulary when talking about cognitive load.

item.title: Developer Community at a Non-Dev Company: a How-to Guide
item.description: Creating a thriving professional community is essential for every workplace. It can be particularly challenging for groups outside a company’s core business areas. I am a software developer passionate about community building who works at an energy company. In this talk, I will reflect on what makes a workplace feel welcoming and inclusive. We will dive into good practices for establishing a dev community within a non-dev company.

item.title: Enter the gray area
item.description: Do you want a super power few others have? A power that helps in meetings, discussions and everyday life? Then we have to travel to the gray area together! The mystical area where we together can become super heroes by learning the power of nuance 🦸

item.title: Frå kontor til kjøkkenbord: fjernarbeid og arbeidsmiljø
item.description: Fjernarbeid har blitt ein stor del av kvardagen for oss som jobbar i IT. Fjenarbeid stiller nye krav til korleis ein organiserer arbeidet, og det påverker arbeidsmiljøet på godt og på vondt. Kva seier forskinga om fjernarbeid, og korleis kan ein få fjernarbeid til å fungere godt i praksis?

item.title: Lunch & drop in yoga
item.description:
Time to fill up on energy and discuss the day so far!

Enjoy our massive buffet both hot and cold with all kinds of cuisines, styles and desserts for days! Vegan options for both too.

Don’t forget Booster has 100 speakers (yes really) and they all love to talk to strangers, so don’t be afraid to approach someone you don’t know and discuss IT.

Need to move around a bit after all the talks and before the food coma hits? Join our daily yoga session the first 30 minutes of every lunch at Dræggen 3.

item.title: Lunch & drop in yoga
item.description:
Time to fill up on energy and discuss the day so far!

Enjoy our massive buffet both hot and cold with all kinds of cuisines, styles and desserts for days! Vegan options for both too.

Don’t forget Booster has 100 speakers (yes really) and they all love to talk to strangers, so don’t be afraid to approach someone you don’t know and discuss IT.

Need to move around a bit after all the talks and before the food coma hits? Join our daily yoga session the first 30 minutes of every lunch at Dræggen 3.

item.title: npm install malware
item.description: Npm is perhaps the biggest spreader of malware targeting developers. I’ll go over how malware can be spread on npm and some ways we can protect ourselves

item.title: People with agendas
item.description: Alexander Hamilton wrote a warning in 1787, that looking for peace and harmony among neighbouring states is to disregard the uniform course of human events. There is a war in Europe, we are in a still escalating hybrid situation with Russia and we know our code and data can and will be weaponized if the powers that be decide to do so. Moreover, big tech are pushing their own agenda, they do not like EU regulations that hit them in the pocket but they do love our money. At the same time they hold incredible power in terms of technology that cannot be easily replaced and enormous amounts of our data that cannot be easily moved. What the f … do we do?

item.title: Tech debt nomads and slash-and-burn development
item.description: Many developers are tech debt nomads. When starting in a new role, we are righteously indignant about the shortcomings of existing solutions, preferring to burn them to the ground and start anew. A couple of years down the road, when the consequences of our own choices start to make themselves manifest and progress slows down, we find it’s time to move on. To replace us come other tech debt nomads, and the process repeats itself. What fuels this process? And what are the effects on the systems we build? On the organizations that own them? On us?

item.title: The Art of Saying It Right
item.description:
Giving feedback the right way can help individuals and teams to identify strengths and areas for improvement, fostering a culture of continuous learning and development.

In this lightning talk, we will explore the BIFF model (Behaviour, Impact, Feelings, Future), a simple and effective framework for feedback by Sarah Cartwright. The BIFF model helps us move from vague criticism to meaningful dialogue, focusing on observable behaviour, describing its impact, expressing feelings, and defining a desired future action. It can be used equally powerful on positive as negative feedback.

item.title: Lunch & drop in yoga
item.description:
Time to fill up on energy and discuss the day so far!

Enjoy our massive buffet both hot and cold with all kinds of cuisines, styles and desserts for days! Vegan options for both too.

Don’t forget Booster has 100 speakers (yes really) and they all love to talk to strangers, so don’t be afraid to approach someone you don’t know and discuss IT.

Need to move around a bit after all the talks and before the food coma hits? Join our daily yoga session the first 30 minutes of every lunch at Dræggen 3.

item.title: Speaker's dinner
item.description:
All speakers are invited to the speakers dinner at Ostra!

Agenda: 18:00 - Drinks and mingling 18:45 - Dinner

Do you have any allergies, intolerances or dietary restrictions? Submit them in Sessionize before 5th of March.

There will be two taxis leaving from the hotel at 17:45 and 18:00.

item.title: Conference dinner
item.description:
You are all invited to the conference dinner at Kulturhuset. Bring your conference badge, as this is your ticket. There is no dress code, so come as you are! 😄

Kulturhuset’s address is: Vaskerelven 8, 5014 Bergen. Look it up on OpenStreetMap or Google Maps.

Do you have any allergies, intolerances or dietary restrictions? Submit them here before 6th of March.

item.title: Ticket information
item.description:
Tickets are sold out!

All tickets grants the attendee access to all three days of the conference and the conference dinner on Wednesday.

Conference Ticket — SOLD OUT

Price: NOK 8500 + MVA

Our regular ticket. This is for attendees whose companies or employers are covering the cost of their conference tickets. You fund a significant part of our non-profit 100% volunteer driven conference.

Individual Nerd Bird Conference Ticket

Price:: NOK 2000 + MVA

item.title: Monetary policy
item.description:
Booster is a non-profit conference, run by a group of volunteers. Our goal is to create the best possible conference for our participants and speakers, and to strengthen the local software development community. Any profits from the conference are used to ensure that we can continue organizing the conference in the following years. We are normally not able to cover travel and lodging for speakers.

There are quite a few exceptions though. We invite a limited number of speakers in order to ensure diversity, and to help market the conference for both submissions and early bird ticket sales. For invited speakers, we refund reasonable travel expenses and provide hotel. We do not pay honorariums for any speakers.

item.title: Partner with Booster
item.description:
Booster is an international IT conference situated in Bergen, and the most important meeting place for the local software development community. Booster is a forum for presentation, debate and study of information technology and agile methods, organized as a three day conference with lectures, workshops and lightning talks of high academic quality.

Booster is aimed at software developers, project managers, testers, designers, architects, security experts and usability professionals. Booster focuses on craftsmanship, methods and quality and facilitates for professional networking.

item.title: Booster Code of Conduct
item.description:
All attendees, speakers, partners, staff and volunteers at our conference are required to follow the code of conduct.

Short Version

Have a good time and help others have a good time too. Say hi to someone you don’t know. Meet interesting people. Don’t act surprised when a person doesn’t conform to a stereotype. No one is at Booster just to represent a minority so start conversations about tech, the weather, the food, the best speaker or the delights of Bergen before talking about “being minority X in tech”. Make sure people are okay with touching before you touch them. Laugh and learn. You, and everyone else, is here to have a good time and learn something new – let us know if anything is getting in the way of that.

item.title: What makes Booster special?
item.description:
Booster is a software conference for the entire team. That means if you’re a developer, project manager, architect, UX professional, tester or security professional, we have something for you!

Our goal is that you come back from the conference with real knowledge you can use. That’s why interactive, hands-on workshops fill up most of the hours of the conference. We also have lightning talks, a few short talks, a couple of keynotes and open spaces.

item.title: AI Customer Delivery Manager @ Pontis Technology
item.description: Domagoj Marić graduated from the Faculty of Electrical Engineering and Computing in Zagreb, where he initially specialized in the field of information security, while towards the end of his studies, he shifted more deeply into the field of data science with a focus on web content extraction (web scraping/crawling). He began his business career at Megatrend poslovna rješenja, where he worked on the development of Python applications and data solutions with a focus on creating virtual assistants and later as the head of the data science department. That role continued through his career at Comping. Today, he works at Pontis Technology as the AI customer delivery manager, leading the delivery of projects in the area of natural language processing, computer vision, predictive analytics and generative AI. In addition to responsibilities in the data science domain, he also has experience and works as a lecturer in the field of programming.

item.title: Alliander - Solution Architect
item.description: Daan van Berkel is a enthusiastic software tinkerer with a knack for presenting technical details in a clear and concise manner.

item.title: Avdelingsleder UX i Kantega
item.description: Siv is a senior interaction designer and has worked with digital user experiences for over 20 years. She is excited about new ways of working with UX in the era of AI. She has recently taken the couses “AI and design thinking” at NTNU and “Responsible AI Leadership at BI. She is excited to share her experiences with you. Siv is manager for User Experience and Design in Kantega.

item.title: AWS, Senior Solutions Architect
item.description: Currently thinking big, inventing and simplifying for my customers as a Senior Solutions Architect in AWS. With a long tenure of working in technologies, from enterprise leadership roles to being a co-founder in my own startup, I have always been a software engineer at heart. Building products to solve customer problems is my craft. I am equally passionate about creating an innovative, and inclusive culture that is grounded in an unrelenting curious mindset.

item.title: Booster Kids
item.description:
For niende gang arrangerer Boosterkonferansen, i samarbeid med Bergen Offentlige Bibliotek, utviklerkonferanse for barn. Konferansen avholdes på Radisson Blu Royal Hotel, Bryggen søndag 15. mars 2026. Det blir kurs for ulike aldersgrupper og et sterkt faglig innhold, akkurat slik en god konferanse skal være!

OBS: Foreldre trenger ikke å booke billetter til seg selv!

Program

11:00 - 12:00: Registrering

12:00 - 13:30: Workshops, del 1

13:30 - 14:15: Lunsj

14:15 - 16:00: Workshops, del 2

item.title: Booster Kids Early Access
item.description:
For niende gang arrangerer Boosterkonferansen, i samarbeid med Bergen Offentlige Bibliotek, utviklerkonferanse for barn. Konferansen avholdes på Radisson Blu Royal Hotel, Bryggen søndag 15. mars 2026. Det blir kurs for ulike aldersgrupper og et sterkt faglig innhold, akkurat slik en god konferanse skal være!

OBS: Foreldre trenger ikke å booke billetter til seg selv!

Program

11:00 - 12:00: Registrering

12:00 - 13:30: Workshops, del 1

13:30 - 14:15: Lunsj

14:15 - 16:00: Workshops, del 2

item.title: CCPO Variant Trondheim
item.description: Malin Karlsen er seniorrådgiver i Variant. Hun har bred erfaring innen både privat og offentlig sektor, og brenner for å bygge bro mellom folk og ulike fagfelt, oftest gjennom strategi, design og prosessledelse.

item.title: CDO
item.description:
Tonje Evanger is Chief Design Officer at Variant. She has spent 18 years leading innovation processes in startups and large corporates, always with a focus on connecting strategy, development, culture and people.
Tonje is also Ass. Professor of Strategic Design and Scenario Development at NTNU, Institute of Design, as she loves to learn and to share learning.

item.title: Chief Engineer at Computas
item.description: Filip van Laenen started his career at Computas in the previous millennium, first as a Java developer, later as a business analyst, IT architect, and various other project roles. He’s guilty of lots of bad drawings, but tries to improve himself, and would like to make the world a better place by helping others to improve too.

item.title: CISO KS Digital
item.description: Kristian used to be a developer, but sometime around 2013 crossed over to security and haven’t really looked back since. He holds a masters degree in Information Security and has worked on security issues in various industries. Currently he works as Chief Information Security Officer in KS Digital and insists that he solves problems, not create them.

item.title: Consultant @ Solea
item.description:
Hi! My name is Mustafa and I’ve been working as a developer for pretty much half my life at this point.

I’m a consultant, working for Solea, where i do mostly front-end development while i also enjoy working with other parts of the stack whenever that’s necessary.

When I’m not working or playing games on the computer, you can find me in the kitchen experimenting with new food recipes or spending time out in nature.

item.title: Consultant at Miles
item.description: Ludvig is a Consultant at Miles Bergen. He has worked several years in the fintech industry where his focus has primarily been to modernize legacy systems.

item.title: CTO @ Frendegruppen
item.description: Jeg har rundt 20 års erfaring innen teknologi, med roller som utvikler, arkitekt, prosjektleder og leder, primært innen bank og finans.

item.title: CTO | Agile Coach | Teamwork Realist
item.description: Øivind Heggland is a Product Owner and CTO at Aquateknikk who knows that “Agile” on a whiteboard doesn’t mean a team actually works together. With a Master’s in Applied Computer Science and hands-on experience building IoT solutions, Azure integrations, and leading digitalization projects, he brings technical credibility to the conversation. Certified PSM II and PSPO II, Øivind has coached teams, facilitated workshops, and seen the patterns that separate real collaboration from the illusion of it. He’s passionate about helping teams move beyond rituals and ticket ownership toward genuine shared accountability.

item.title: Curious tinkerer and senior consultant at Apparat AS
item.description: Ricco Førgaard is a senior software developer and consultant at Apparat, focusing on the systems and infrastructure that keep applications running smoothly and reliably.

item.title: Designer at Variant Bergen
item.description:
Mari recently graduated with a master’s degree in Interaction Design from NTNU, where she explored how design can help people make more energy-conscious decisions. Through her thesis work, she spent countless hours involving users, testing ideas, and uncovering what actually motivates people to change behavior, and what is just distracting.

She now works as a designer and consultant at Variant, helping teams create meaningful, user-centered digital experiences. Mari is passionate about sustainability, systems thinking, and the small design choices that can drive big impact. When she’s not designing, she’s probably on a ferry, still thinking about energy.

item.title: Designer i Sikt - kunnskapssektorens tjenesteleverandør
item.description: Arman Bulak Hagelia er designer i Sikt hvor han jobber med studieadministrative brukere. Dette er løsninger som har flere tusen bruker hver dag, så han jobber for at karbonavtrykket skal være så lavt som mulig.

item.title: Designer og fagleder i Bekk
item.description: Hanna jobber som designer i Bekk, hvor hun spesialiserer seg på datavisualisering og bærekraft. Hun brenner for å gjøre komplekse data forståelige gjennom visuelle løsninger som engasjerer og informerer.

item.title: Developer @ Solea
item.description: Er ikke mye å skrive her.

item.title: Developer @ Tv2
item.description: Done some sort of programming most of my life, roots back to c64/amiga. Avid linux, neovim user and prefer to work in the terminal whenever I have the choice

item.title: Developer @mnemonic
item.description: I am a front-end engineer specializing in technologies like React and TypeScript. I have a passion for tracking down tricky bugs and solving complex issues that require intense teamwork. Currently, I work at mnemonic, focusing on the core functionality of our Argus platform, where I help build secure, reliable front-end solutions.

item.title: Developer & Security Engineer at Kantega SSO
item.description:
Developer & Security Engineer at Kantega SSO, engineering digital identity standards for secure authentication to the Atlassian ecosystem while facilitating and promoting secure software development practices. Contributing author of the OWASP AI exchange and creator of the threat modeling game Elevation of MLsec.

M. Sc. in Computer Science at NTNU, researching usage of static analysis tools like Spotbugs to find vulnerabilities in OpenID Connect client implementations. Passionate about software security, music and rock climbing.

item.title: Developer at Computas
item.description: Ingrid is a fullstack developer from Bergen, working as a consultant at Computas. She has an M.Sc. in Computer Science from NTNU, and has been working for 1.5 years. Ingrid has a passion for problem-solving and learning new technologies.

item.title: Developer at Elmera
item.description: Systemutvikler og vintage webdesignentusiast.

item.title: Developer, NRK
item.description: Heidi is a developer and technical coach, with over 15 years of experience in software development. She is enthusiastic about most aspects of software development, and is especially interested in functional programming and how we can work better together as a team.

item.title: Developer, team lead and mentor
item.description: Siv loves creating software, preferably together with other people. She works both as a developer, mentor and team lead, and has been teaching and mentoring for over 20 years. The passion for teaching and outreach led her to help found the Nerdschool meetup, help create technical onboarding courses and teach fundamental software engineering skills at the University of Bergen. She is actively engaged in teaching newcomers to our field about craftsmanship of developing software and also spends time encouraging girls to choose tech and IT as a career path.

item.title: Development team lead
item.description: I have worked in the software industry since 2003. I started as a fullstack developer, and have transitioned into management since 2020. I now work as a product development manager for eApply in Solea AS. My passion is building teams that thrive together, and I love it when people challenge the way we work and encourage change!

item.title: DevOps Engineer, Fjord Line AS
item.description: Talina Vaterlaus is a DevOps Specialist at Fjord Line AS with a past in IT Operations, a former life as a PR consultant, and a stint in the Swiss military to round it all out. After years of keeping systems from crashing, she now automates infrastructure, wrangles CI/CD pipelines, and keeps cloud environments and Kubernetes clusters from spiraling into chaos. She prefers her infrastructure reliable, her tooling sharp, and her logs just chatty enough to flag when something’s off.

item.title: Digital marketer
item.description: Kathrine is a digital marketer at Knowit Experience Bergen. She works with marketing, SEO, and content in an era where AI is reshaping search and visibility. She is particularly interested in how new tools can be used without losing the human perspective.

item.title: Director of Technology at Knowit Experience Bergen
item.description: André is the Director of Technology at Knowit Experience Bergen. He is an enthusiastic and experienced technology leader and agilist with a strong background in software development. Before moving to a management role, he spent more than a decade as a software developer. André was part of the team establishing the Booster conference and helped organize the conference from 2011-2022.

item.title: Domain-Driven Design Coach at CoWork, Norway
item.description: Mufrid is devoted to leading and coaching product development teams, by cultivating knowledge sharing in the organization and increasing co-developer’s business understanding. His professional philosophy is rooted in Domain-Driven Design and in building agile and technical coaching culture on an organization-wide level. With 20+ years of experience as software architect, developer and technical coach, Mufrid has been deeply involved in developing enterprise-solutions in complex domains, such as healthcare, insurance and telecom.

item.title: Feedback Evangelist and founder of Feedbach
item.description: I am a happy guy who has had most roles within software development, and most recently I have founded a company and launched a product (in 30 days!). These days I’m evangelising over feedback and why it is so important if you are into continuous improvement!

item.title: Fostering Team Spirit in the World of Tech at Webstep
item.description: After many years of studying psychology in London, so far as to get a doctorate in the field, Cathrine decided to move back to Norway and cultivate her interest in technology. In Webstep, she stands as an arbiter, managing consultants and the vast spectrum of tech services they provide for their clients. Cathrine has always shown to have a keen interest in team harmony and productivity, while always staying on top of developments at the crossroads of tech and psychology.

item.title: Fractional CTO
item.description: Anders has been working as an architect, developer, and technology leader since 2009.

item.title: Frontend developer at Rubberduck
item.description:
An enthusiastic accessibility advocate, usually talking about WCAG, WAI-ARIA, A11Y and other abbreviations.

As a frontend developer, Vegard enjoys working on the “front of frontend”, in the intersection between programming and UX, and his goal is that the services he works on should be accessible to everyone, regardless of their preferred platform or their abilities.

After recently building welfare services and design systems at Nav for a few years, he currently enjoys being able to talk about the weather all day at work while building Yr.no.

item.title: Frontend developer in Variant
item.description:
Elise is a frontend developer in Variant and has been involved in a diverse range of projects for seven years. As a consultant, she has worked in and out of various teams, experiencing a wide range of team compositions.

She has a special interest and passion for accessibility.

She enjoys sharing insights about observations in the industry, and in 2022, she was the “Voice of the Year” in kode24, an online newspaper for Norwegian developers.

item.title: Frontend-consultant
item.description: Frontend developer working for Miles in Bergen, with great interest in user design and accessibility.

item.title: Fullstack developer - Zrch AS
item.description: Jostein is a full-stack developer who loves exploring new technologies. He has experience with everything from backend development in Rust, Java and .NET to frontend development in React and mobile development in React Native. Jostein enjoys teaching and has previously lectured on several subjects at NTNU. He currently works with the real estate platform Hjem.no 🏠

item.title: Fullstack Developer at Variant Bergen
item.description:
Tuva is an enthusiastic book nerd who never grows tired of hearing about your favourite read(s). Fiction or not🐉📚

She has a masters degree in computer science from NTNU in Trondheim and specialises in software systems. Today she works as a developer and consultant at Variant in Bergen.

Lately Tuva has been working on improving Variants own website, where she has gotten to challenge herself by interacting with Sanity’s content management system while exploring her passion for frontend development!<3 Every day is a battle against new and interesting bugs!🐛

item.title: Fullstack Software Engineer and Head of Guild Programming @ Computas
item.description:
Ahmed is a fullstack developer at Computas. He has a M.Sc. in Computer Science: Digital Economy and Management from the University of Oslo.

In his work as a Software Engineer at Computas, Ahmed has also become one of the leaders of the Guild Programming community at Computas. This role includes hosting internal conferences, hosting programming nights and being an enabler for knowledge sharing in the company.

item.title: Grunder av Fjelltoppjakten
item.description: Torkel Velure har lang erfaring med apputvikling og har vært med på utvikling av apper for blant annet DNB, Sbanken, Cutters, Nordea og Rema 1000. Samtidig som han utvikler apper på fritiden, som Fjelltoppjakten og pokset.com.

item.title: Head of DevOps
item.description: Passionate about advancing the DevOps philosophy, I remain a hands-on developer dedicated to fostering collaboration, improving delivery practices, and building resilient systems.

item.title: Head of digital development at Fjord Line AS
item.description: IT professional, proficient in Azure, Kubernetes, CI/CD, and cloud solutions. With a focus on DevOps and full-stack development, he strives to merge coding and operations seamlessly

item.title: Head of UX and Design, Kraftlauget Øst
item.description: Øyvind Nordseth Westbye is a UX and design leader with 15+ years of experience turning complex systems into products people actually understand. With a background that straddles computer science, consultancy and design leadership, he’s mildly obsessed with how we talk to users – and how easily we fool ourselves when we don’t. At Booster he’ll share his hard-earned lessons on story-based interviewing, honest discovery, and avoiding the trap of building beautifully polished, completely wrong features.

item.title: Host of Tech Won’t Save Us
item.description: Paris Marx is a Canadian tech critic and host of the award-winning Tech Won’t Save Us podcast. He speaks around the world about the politics of technology and co-authored a white paper called Reclaiming Digital Sovereignty. Paris is the author of “Road to Nowhere” and his next book “Hyperscale” comes out later this year.

item.title: Human
item.description: Johannes is a full stack (read: frontend) developer with 10+ years of experience. He has probably installed millions of npm packages.

item.title: I create kick-ass user experiences at Troms Fylkeskommune
item.description: Abel is a software developer turned UX designer to create kick-ass user experiences at Troms County. He has a passion for innovation, collaboration, and design systems, bridging the gap between design and development to bring his creations to life.

item.title: Infra Developer, Frende Forsikring
item.description: I enjoy all things linux and open source, and am a huge proponent for self-hosting.

item.title: IoT developer at Prelect
item.description: Just a software developer chasing cool tech and interesting problems. Currently doing so by accelerating electrification at Prelect.

item.title: Kantega, Data Engineer
item.description: Viktor is a Data Engineer specializing in data platforms and AI search.

item.title: Kantega, Systemutvikler
item.description: Dani begynte karrieren som lektor i musikkpedagogikk, men fant ut at kaffen var bedre i det private næringsliv.

item.title: Keeping it simple and useful for decades.
item.description: Martin is a senior consultant at Kantega in Bergen. These days we works mostly with devops and the backend. But with almost 35 years in the industry, he has been involved in most areas of software development.

item.title: KI lead og avdelingsleder i Kantega
item.description: Som leder for KI i Kantega kombinerer Nora tung teknisk spisskompetanse med en unik evne til å drive teknologisk og organisatorisk endring. Med erfaring fra utvikling av både dataplattformer og avanserte KI-løsninger for kunder som TV2, Enova, Bergen kommune og BKK, hjelper hun virksomheter med å realisere verdien i egne data. Hennes bakgrunn som keeper på toppnivå har gitt henne en sjelden evne til å prestere under press og se potensialet i både mennesker og teknologi.

item.title: Kraftlauget, Developer,
item.description: Helge har arbeida som teamlead, arkitekt og utviklar på store og virksomhetskritiske applikasjonar i ei årrekkje. Han har spesiell kompetanse innan frontendutvikling og korleis ein skal implementere forretningsverdi i teknologiske løysingar.

item.title: Lead UX researcher @ Expedition UX
item.description: Kim Feenstra Kuiper is a seasoned UX researcher with experience at companies like Spotify, Goodnotes, and King (makers of Candy Crush). Through her consultancy, Expedition UX, she helps product teams go beyond usability testing—using deep, practical research methods to understand real user behavior. Kim is passionate about making UX research accessible for cross-functional teams and embedding it in fast-paced product development to create products people truly want to use.

item.title: Partners
item.description:
Our 31 partners for 2026

Thank you to all our essential partners. Each one gets among other things co-branding and 3 tickets. Therefore they participate, learn and network on the floor with all the attendees. The conference simply cannot be the same without them! Become one of our partners.

item.title: Principal consultant - Capgemini
item.description: For over 25 years, Lars has been in the trenches building software for demanding industries like energy, telecom, and healthcare. His journey has taken him from co-founding startups to Capgemini Bergen where he now heads the development team. He thrives at the intersection of deep technology and smart strategy, with a passion for building things that actually work.

item.title: Product advisor @ Kraftlauget
item.description: Hi! I’m a product manager gone product advisor. I’m passionate about building the right stuff and making teams tick.

item.title: Product manager and synthetic data expert, Nav IT
item.description: Inger is an interaction designer, developer and e-government specialist with experience across privacy-by-design, user-centered development, and legal technology. After several years of working as an IT-consultant, she is stepping into the role of product manager and domain advisor for Team Dolly, where she oversees the development of synthetic data solutions that enable safer, more efficient innovation across Nav’s systems.

item.title: Produktområdearkitekt, TV 2
item.description: Karina har jobbet som utvikler og arkitekt siden 2017, og har de siste 6 årene jobbet hos TV 2. Selv som arkitekt har hun alltid tid til å prioritere utvikling i sin arbeidshverdag. Å kunne dykke ned i store problemstillinger, få hendene skitne, se selv hva som berører seg i det teknologiske landskapet, og samtidig kunne drøfte og tenke arkitektur for fremtiden, er noe hun elsker ved jobben sin.

item.title: Professor of Computer Science Education
item.description:
