Felienne Hermans is a professor of Computer Science Education at the Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam. She also works as a high-school CS teacher one day a week at Open Schoolgemeenschap Bijlmer.

Felienne is the creator of the Hedy programming language, a gradual and multi-lingual programming language designed for teaching and author of the book “The Programmer’s Brain” a book that helps programmers understand how their brain works and how to use it more effectively. In 2021, Felienne was awarded the Dutch Prize for ICT research.

item.title: Retrospective
item.description:
Thank you for a great 2026!
See you March 17-19 2027

We’ve had 100+ speakers, 300+ attendees, 23 workshops, 55 talks and 3 keynotes over 3 wonderful days.

A very important thanks to our 31(!) partners.

Special thanks to our 22 volunteers and all our local suppliers: Sjur Pollen, Henrik Skauge, Kevin Andreas Smilden, Jan Magne Hanssen, Christian Reiertsen, Marken Kaffe, Tedragen, Hallaisen, Fjåk, Kulturhuset, Ostra and Radisson Blu Royal Bergen.

Don’t forget, videos from all the talks are available at the program page.

item.title: Seksjonsleder i NAV
item.description: Louis er seksjonsleder for plattform i NAV. Hans motivasjon at folk får ytelsene og hjelpen de skal ha raskt og riktig. Det klarer vi bare om vi har gode plattformtjenester slik at teamene kan tenke på andre ting enn Kubernetes.

item.title: Senior advicer and responsible for synthetic data, Nav IT
item.description: Anders has more than 40 years experince with software development both for private companies and government agencies. Over the last 5 years he has become central in the national community for managing synthetic data - “TestNorge-samarbeidet”

item.title: Senior consultant at Miles AS
item.description: Sveinung works as a consultant for Miles. He has a broad background from systems development, team leadership, technology leadership and product development. Within Miles he works with a small group keep (and improve) Miles as a learning organization.

item.title: Senior Engineer
item.description:
Michael has been writing software for more than 20 years.

He founded a small company at 15 years old, studied physics, holds a PhD in mathematics, and is an expert on xkcd comics.

His work experience includes numerical optimization, project management, consulting, data science, and a significant amount of debugging.

He also hosts the Rust user group in Bergen.

item.title: Senior fullstack utvikler i Kantega
item.description: Medeier og senior utvikler i Kantega. Jordomseiler. Norges tredje beste javaprogrammerer.

item.title: Senior Software Developer, Mimir
item.description: Julian works as a Senior Software Developer at Mimir. Over the years he has gathered experience in a variety of different languages and technologies. As a professional developer he has mainly worked in the domains of video processing and seismic data acquisition. When given the option Julian’s language of choice is Rust.

item.title: Senior tjenestedesigner i Youwell
item.description: Sofija Ivanova er senior tjenestedesigner i Youwell, hvor hun jobber med digitale løsninger som støtter atferdsendring og mestring i helsesektoren. Hun har over ti års erfaring som konsulent fra Knowit Experience og Bouvet, og sitter i styret for IxDA Bergen.

item.title: Senior UX designer @ Forte
item.description: Nadia er en erfaren senior UX-designer som ser mønstre der andre ser rot. Hun liker å finne systemer i kaos og lar seg lett fascinere av det som irriterer.

item.title: Senior UX designer i Kantega
item.description: Siri har 13 års erfaring som designer og har jobbet hele karrieren i tverrfaglige team, blant annet i DNB og i SATS. Hun trives best når hun kan jobbe tett med brukere og kan ta beslutninger basert på både kvalitativ og kvantitativ innsikt.

item.title: Senior UX designer i Kantega
item.description: Sofie er en UX-designer med lidenskap for å skape brukervennlige og estetiske digitale løsninger. Hun legger stor vekt på tett samarbeid og åpen dialog mellom design og utvikling, fordi gode løsninger oppstår når ulike perspektiver forstår hverandre.

item.title: Senior UX-designer from Netlife Design in Oslo
item.description:
Daniel is a UX designer working for Netlife Design in Oslo, Norway. He has worked with national and international customers such as Telenor, Norwegian, Gjensidige, the Norwegian Labour and Welfare Administration and the Norwegian Digitalisation Agency, helping people with everything from buying their plane tickets to getting child benefit from the government.

Daniel is passionate about diversity and sustainability in design, and strongly believes that the people who create digital products and services need to work harder to make sure that what we make is good both for the people using them, and for the world.

item.title: Seniorkonsulent i Miles
item.description: Seniorkonsulent i Miles

item.title: Seniorkonsulent og medeier hos Kantega
item.description: Kombinasjonen av en mastergrad i Informatikk/Optimering og praktisk erfaring med alt fra backend og API-design til UX og designsystem gir Brage et unikt perspektiv til å trekke linjer på tvers av fagdisipliner som ofte står litt for langt fra hverandre.

item.title: Software developer
item.description: Einar W. Høst is a software developer at Norkart. He enjoys collaborative modelling, API design and computer programming. He has a PhD in Computer Science from the University of Oslo.

item.title: Software developer
item.description: Embla is a fullstack developer with five years experience from a variety of projects in the consulting industry. She has a background from aquaculture, but found her passion for developing while talking an intro course to objective programming. Currently, she is working as a senior consultant at Sonat.

item.title: Software Developer and Tech Lead @ Unfold/NoA
item.description: Ekaterina Orlova is a passionate front-end developer dedicated to creating beautiful and accessible digital experiences. Armed with degrees in both philosophy and programming, she delights in exploring the deeper meanings behind her code—because who doesn’t love a good existential question between debug sessions? As a certified Scrum Master, she thrives on agile methodologies that fuel collaboration and innovation. With a portfolio that includes major clients like Telenor, KLP, and now OBOS, she navigates the tech landscape with a blend of skill and humor. A champion for diversity and inclusion, Ekaterina has led initiatives for women in tech, organized workshops on accessibility, and proudly served as a former co-organizer of the Mobile Era conference. When she’s not immersed in code or sharing her insights at tech conferences, she’s keeping up with the delightful chaos brought on by her two energetic kids.

item.title: Software Developer at Equinor
item.description:
With a background in Applied Mathematics (MSc) and Reservoir Simulation (PhD), I’m currently a Principal Software Engineer at Equinor.

I’m leading a project with industry-transforming potential in Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS). We’re developing an open-source platform, AcidWatch, that enables researchers to analyze CO2 impurities and mitigate corrosion risks 🌍 Check it out here: https://acidwatch.radix.equinor.com/

When I’m not busy saving the planet one line of code at a time, you can find me honing my public speaking skills 🎤 (watch out, TED stage!). I’m also passionate about community building and organizing conferences that foster collaboration🤝

item.title: Software developer at Noria
item.description: Rudi is a consultant at Noria with a master’s degree in software development from the University of Bergen. He has a great interest in learning new technologies, languages, and not least how to use them to improve the developer experience.

item.title: Software Engineer at AWS
item.description: Rico is a developer on the AWS CDK team, and is constantly disappointed to discover tasks computers haven’t automated away yet.

item.title: Storebrand, Security Assurance Manager
item.description: Oleksandr Kazymyrov is a seasoned cybersecurity professional with a hacker’s mindset and a passion for offensive security. He holds a Ph.D. in cryptography and has over 15 years of experience in information security. As the Security Assurance Manager at Storebrand, Oleksandr leverages his offensive expertise to ensure the company’s security controls are robust and continuously improving. Previously, he built and led Storebrand’s red team program, uncovering critical vulnerabilities and strengthening the organization’s defenses. This unique blend of academic rigor and hands-on offensive experience allows him to bridge the gap between offense and defense, driving a more resilient security posture

item.title: System Architect at TRYG Forsikring
item.description: Gaurav is a System Architect with more than 15 years of experience designing and delivering scalable enterprise software systems. He specializes in Java, Spring Boot, cloud-native architectures, and Kubernetes-based platforms. He is passionate about building resilient solutions and sharing practical insights on software architecture, cloud technologies, and emerging innovation trends.

item.title: T-shaped person
item.description: Øystein Øvrebø is a tech lead at Sopra Steria in Bergen, Norway. He has worked on creating software in teams since back in the olden (but not golden) days when Java was still in version 1. Remember when XML was cool? He does. And he might blabber on endlessly about it if you will let him. You should not.

item.title: Teamlead, teamcoach, Kantega AS
item.description: As an IT team lead/team coach Maja is known for her caring and compassionate way of relating to others, and her engaging way of inspiring her co-workers. She thinks the magic lies in the teams ability to cooperate and feel safe with each other and is eager to facilitate this whenever she gets the opportunity to do so. She is an avid supporter of the idea that the client/supplier relationship greatly benefits a partner mind-set where both parties share risk and trust. Since 2002 she has played different roles in the team management universe and worked in many different types of teams, with many different type of clients.

item.title: Teamleader og utvikler av hjerte
item.description: Anders har over 20 års erfaring som utvikler og teamleder, og brenner for smidig utvikling, DevOps og mikrotjenester. Han er like opptatt av personlig utvikling og teamdynamikk som av teknologi, og liker å bygger sterke, autonome team gjennom et tillitsbasert og coachende lederskap.

item.title: Tech lead at Plug
item.description: Håvard is a Tech Lead at Plug, where he works to make shipping greener. He has worked with organizations such as the Directorate of Fisheries, TV 2, and Fjordkraft, and previously at Vizrt in Bergen and Zurich. He is passionate about cloud technologies, Kubernetes, and automation.

item.title: Technical architect at Frende Forsikring
item.description: David is a technical architect that works on solutions from top to bottom, from customer value to bytes on disk. He believes deep technical knowledge is essential for solving hard technical problems like legacy, scalability, quality and more.

item.title: Technology Coach and Advisor
item.description:
Chris is a technology coach and advisor helping technology teams drive business success. His focus is on helping startups realise their vision and new CTOs flourish in their roles. He also supports executives & boards with strategic technology advice, and engineering teams with training, mentoring and consulting in architecture, quality, domain-driven design and test driven development.

He is a regular meetup & conference speaker (https://chrissimon.au/speaking/) and to support teams using Domain-Driven Design, he recently launched https://contextive.tech & co-founded the DDD Australia meetup and ADAConf (https://adaconf.org) conferences.

item.title: Teknisk leder innen dataplattform, Miles
item.description:
Peter er teknisk leder og data platform engineer som har erfaring fra Equinor, Miljødirektoratet og Politiets IT-enhet

Han er en kulturbygger, og har jobbet som scrum master, team leder og er en entusiast for at team skal fungere optimalt og effektivt.

Utenom jobb er Peter en som elsker variasjon og utfordring. Han er helst å finne i hengende i tynne tau langt oppe i fjellveggen om sommeren eller i bratte fjellsider på vinteren.

item.title: Test lead at Systek
item.description:
Cecilie is a Test Lead at Systek with a technical background. She’s been working with test in both the public and private sector for the last decade and likes to share her ideas at conferences.

Cecilie believes the quality of the system is the responsibility of the entire team and not something you leave to testers alone. She loves working in agile teams, is a proponent of automating all manual processes, and has a special interest in making applications accessible for all.

item.title: Tjenestedesigner og leder for design
item.description: Anne er tjenestedesigner og leder for design i Liflig med 17 års erfaring som konsulent. Hun er levende opptatt av å finne de gode løsningene på brukernes reelle behov , samt å tro på det beste i mennesker.

item.title: Utviklar hos KS Digital

item.title: Utvikler hos Sikt - kunnskapssektorens tjenesteleverandør
item.description: Solveig har jobbet som full-stack utvikler hos Sikt i over 2 år nå. Til daglig arbeider hun med utviklingen av Læremiddelkatalogen - en oversikt over alle digitale læremidler i grunnopplæringen. Selv om Solveig arbeider full-stack har hun en forkjærlighet for frontend og gode brukeropplevelser.

item.title: Utvikler og Tech lead hos KS Digital
item.description: Hege har en doktorgrad i eksperimentell kjernefysikk, men er konvertert utvikler og jobber nå som backend-utvikler og tech-lead hos KS Ditgital. Ho er også en av initiativtagerne bak SheCodes.

item.title: UX Designer and Head of Marketing at Kraftlauget
item.description: I am a UX designer who thrives at the intersection of strategic design, communication, and business development. With experience in service design, prototyping, interaction design, and user insights, I’m passionate about creating inclusive solutions and effective processes that bring people together around shared goals.

item.description: Helene er en UX’er som er opptatt at vi setter brukerne i sentrum av designprosessen for å skape gode digitale løsninger.

item.title: UX Designer i Kraftlauget
item.description: Cathrine er UX Designer i konsulentselskapet Kraftlauget, med 12 års erfaring og en mastergrad i informasjonsvitenskap fra Universitetet i Bergen. Hun har bred kompetanse innen både brukertesting og tjenestedesign. Cathrine brenner for å utvikle bærekraftige, skalerbare løsninger, og er spesielt engasjert i Figma og Figma Make. Hun er kjent for sitt smittende gode humør, og setter pris på både faglig samarbeid og en uformell tone i prosjektene.

item.title: UX designer, Sikt - Kunnskapssektorens tjenesteleverandør
item.description: Mayli Munkebye er UX-designer i Sikt, der hun for tiden jobber med Nasjonalt vitenarkiv (NVA) – et nasjonalt system for publisering av offentlig finansiert forskning. I tillegg er hun en aktiv del av Sikts faggruppe for bærekraft, hvor hun brenner for å øke bevisstheten rundt hvordan designere og uvtiklere kan skape løsninger som er mindre energikrevende og mer miljøvennlige.

item.title: UX Researcher at Citi
item.description:
Sophie is a UX Researcher at Citi with a background in Chemical Engineering. She has built and scaled research practices in both start-ups and enterprises, embedding human-centred ways of working that drive real business impact.

At the crossroads of design, technology and strategy, she transforms research into a strategic partner. She combines scientific thinking with creativity to tackle complex challenges and uncover new opportunities.

She also mentors emerging tech talent, championing inclusivity and inspiring the next generation

item.title: UX-designer i Knowit
item.description: Liva er en UX-designer som er spesielt opptatt av å skape inkluderende, brukersentrerte, bærekraftige og robuste tjenester som står seg over tid.

item.title: UX-designer i Kraftlauget
item.description: Jeg er en designer med tyngden innen interaksjonsdesign, som også har stor interesse for teamkultur og kommunikasjon.

item.title: UX-Designer i Kraftlauget
item.description: Jeg er en UX og visuell designer som trives best i krysningspunktet hvor tjenestedesign og UX møter teknologi, alltid med brukeren i sentrum.

item.title: Wulff Consulting AS
item.description: Kristin er rådgiver i Wulff Consulting AS. Hun er opptatt av det systemiske, dvs. at vi trener på å se, og forbedre, strukturer, prosesser og rammebetingelser. Målet hennes er å bidra til at flest mulige folk får en bra arbeidshverdag, derfor brenner hun for å gi team gode rammevilkår slik at de igjen kan levere produkter som gjør hverdagen lettere for brukerne. Hun har hovedfag i psykologi (1996), en master i organisasjon og ledelse (2014). Fra 2018-2022 tok hun en doktorgrad ved NTNU, på hva slags autonomi digitale produktutviklingsteam trenger. Kristin har jobbet innenfor programvareutvikling siden 1998. Fra 2006-2025 jobbet hun i IT-konsulentselskapet Kantega som rådgiver, personalleder på forskjellige nivå, de siste årene som leder for organisasjonsutvikling.
